events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess gas piping residential missing sediment trap/drip leg. Codes IFGC/NFPA 54 require sediment trap/drip leg at appliance where gas is supplied to protect from debris. Missing at rough-complete is non-compliant, fail. Risk maybe medium/high? Gas piping defect can cause appliance malfunction, flame rollout, etc. Since not in service yet, risk high? Need decide. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 70. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "", order [], workable []. findings: missing sediment trap/drip leg at gas appliance. actions: install proper sediment trap/drip leg per code/manufacturer. rationale. references: IFGC 408.4? NFPA 54 7.13? Let's recall. IFGC 2018 Section 408.4 Sediment trap. Where a sediment trap is not incorporated as part of the gas utilization equipment, a sediment trap shall be installed downstream of the equipment shutoff valve as close to the inlet of the equipment as practical. NFPA 54 9.6.7? Actually NFPA 54 2012 9.6.7 Sediment Trap. Need exact-ish. Could reference IFGC 408.4 and NFPA 54 9.6.7. Let's craft JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

  "rationale": "IFGC and NFPA 54 require a sediment trap/drip leg at gas utilization equipment unless the equipment has an integral trap. The observed rough-in lacks this protection, so the installation is non-compliant and must be corrected before close-out.",
  "references": ["IFGC 408.4", "NFPA 54 9.6.7"]
}
